_comment: Design-QA test slate. Published via scripts/publish-test-proposals.mjs. Every title is prefixed 'Test:' so the slate is easy to find and delete from the admin dashboard when testing is done. Deadlines are relative ('+15m', '+2h', '+10d') and resolved at publish time — the short ones close quickly so the Results tab gets real closed proposals.

proposals.title: Test: Should this proposal close within the hour?
proposals.description: Design QA — closes ~15 minutes after publishing. Use it to watch a proposal move from OPEN (live tally, minutes-level countdown) to the CLOSED section of Results. With only a handful of ballots it should close as BELOW THRESHOLD — count shown, split never.

proposals.title: Test: Does the closing-tonight strip work?
proposals.description: Design QA — closes ~2 hours after publishing, so it should appear in the Home closing strip and show an hours-level mono countdown in Results and on its card.

proposals.title: Test: Zero ballots — who casts the first?
proposals.description: Design QA — a fresh proposal with no ballots. The card and detail must show the 25-dot threshold row with 'Be the first verified ballot' and never an empty tally bar. Cast a few ballots to watch the dots fill (3 of 25, 4 of 25…).

proposals.title: Test: A second open question for the queue
proposals.description: Design QA — keeps the vote queue populated so the M2 review session (progress counter, skip, X1 confirm sheet, gold seal) can be exercised more than once.

proposals.title: Test: Multiple choice — which option wins?
proposals.description: Design QA — X2 multiple-choice ballot. Radio selection, pick one of four, mandatory confirm before casting, results on the detail screen.

proposals.title: Test: Ranked choice — order your priorities
proposals.description: Design QA — screen 07 ranked ballot. Tap to rank all four options; the first choice takes the gold badge; reorder freely until you cast.
options: Bike lanes on every arterial road

proposals.title: Test: Alberta-scoped — does scope filtering work?
proposals.description: Design QA — restricted to verified Alberta residents. Albertans see it in the queue with a provincial scope chip; everyone else should only find it in Across Canada as WATCH-ONLY.

proposals.title: Test: Calgary-scoped — municipal level
proposals.description: Design QA — restricted to verified Calgary residents, for the municipal scope chip and the Home level-split bar's municipal segment.
